---
title: "Methods in brief"
---

A one-page, plain-language recap. The full theory, proofs, and implementation
are in the paper and its Web Appendix.

## The problem

Targeted maximum likelihood estimation (TMLE) pairs machine learning with
semiparametric efficiency theory to estimate causal effects. Its standard theory
assumes **independent observations** — but complex surveys (like NHANES) have
**stratification, clustering, and unequal selection**. Treating survey data as
i.i.d. gets both pieces wrong:

- ignoring the **weights** biases the point estimate (it targets a sample, not
  the population, quantity);
- ignoring the **clustered design** understates the variance, so confidence
  intervals are too narrow.

## The estimator

We develop a **survey-aware TMLE** for the population average treatment effect.
Its variance is obtained by **Taylor-series linearization** of the estimator's
influence function, treating the **primary sampling unit (PSU)** as the
independent replication unit. Within explicit conditions, the estimator is
asymptotically normal and the linearization variance is design-consistent under
a stratified multistage design.

## The central result: cross-fitting restores valid inference

With flexible machine-learning nuisances, valid variance **turns on
cross-fitting at the PSU level**: among the estimators evaluated, only
out-of-fold (cross-fitted) fitting keeps coverage valid. A single-fit survey
TMLE can severely under-cover once the learners cross a complexity (Donsker)
boundary — and
internal cluster-aware cross-validation does **not** substitute for
cross-fitting. The [simulation](simulation.qmd) shows this directly: coverage
collapses at the interpolating rung for single-fit, while the cross-fitted arm
stays near nominal.

## The five arms

| Arm | Weights | Variance | Nuisance fitting |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Non-Aware** | ignored | i.i.d. | single-fit |
| **Partially-Aware** | used | i.i.d. | single-fit |
| **Fully-Aware** | used | design-based | single-fit |
| **Fully-Aware-CV** | used | design-based | internal cluster-CV |
| **Fully-Aware-CF** *(recommended)* | used | design-based | **PSU-level cross-fitting** |

`Fully-Aware-CF` is the arm the theory covers for machine-learning nuisances.
